source: ICH S6(R1), 2012; Nishimoto & Kishimoto, 2006; Tanaka et al., 2012 PMC3306224; Rossi et al., 2015, AACR; Tocilizumab SmPC
description: Expected findings for anti-IL-6 and anti-IL-6R monoclonal antibodies (tocilizumab, sarilumab, siltuximab). IL-6 blockade produces a characteristic signature: acute phase protein suppression, mild neutropenia, lipid changes, and albumin normalization. Cynomolgus monkey is the primary pharmacologically relevant species. Inherits Fc-mediated base findings from general_mab.

expected_findings.description: CRP suppression (pharmacodynamic endpoint of IL-6R blockade)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: N/A — complete suppression is the expected PD endpoint. Adversity relates only to downstream infection risk, not CRP level itself.
expected_findings.species_note: Cynomolgus is primary species. Rat may cross-react with surrogate compound. Rabbit cross-reactivity generally absent for human IL-6R-specific mAbs.
expected_findings.rationale: IL-6 is the dominant regulator of hepatic CRP production. Suppression is the PD marker of effective IL-6R blockade. (Nishimoto & Kishimoto, 2006; Tanaka et al., 2012 PMC3306224)

expected_findings.description: Serum amyloid A reduction (acute phase protein suppression)
expected_findings.typical_magnitude: Rapid suppression within 2 days; mirrors CRP kinetics
expected_findings.rationale: SAA is an IL-6-driven acute phase protein; decreases alongside CRP with anti-IL-6 therapy. (Rossi et al., 2015, AACR)
IL-6 suppresses albumin synthesis; anti-IL-6 reverses this. Serum albumin increases as acute phase response resolves. (Rossi et al., 2015, AACR)

expected_findings.description: Neutropenia (mild, expected pharmacology of IL-6 blockade)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3 (ANC <1.0 x 10^9/L) = adverse threshold. Grade 4 (ANC <0.5 x 10^9/L) = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: IL-6 promotes granulopoiesis. Blockade produces pharmacologically expected mild neutropenia; severe neutropenia increases infection risk = adverse. (Tocilizumab SmPC; ICH S6(R1))

expected_findings.description: Total cholesterol increase (reversal of IL-6-driven lipoprotein catabolism)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: >2x ULN sustained or Grade 3 dyslipidemia = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: IL-6 induces VLDL receptor expression increasing LDL-C consumption. Anti-IL-6 reverses this, raising total cholesterol. (Nishimoto et al., PMC3306224)
Same mechanism as cholesterol increase — reversal of IL-6-driven lipoprotein metabolism. (Nishimoto et al., PMC3306224)

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >3x ULN = adverse. >5x ULN = never reclassify (Hy's Law range).
expected_findings.rationale: Noted as expected pharmacology-adjacent effect in clinical trials; mechanism unclear but seen in context of immune modulation. (Tocilizumab SmPC)
Parallels ALT pattern in anti-IL-6 context. (Tocilizumab SmPC)

expected_findings.description: Lymphoid hyperplasia (spleen, lymph nodes) from cytokine modulation
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Lymphoma-like transformation or >Grade 3 severity = never reclassify
expected_findings.rationale: Expected immune organ response to cytokine modulation. (General toxicological pathology)

expected_findings.description: Lymphocyte count shift (CD4+ T-cell and Th17 modulation)
expected_findings.rationale: IL-6 drives Th17 differentiation. Blockade shifts T-cell balance; downstream immunophenotyping changes expected. (Tocilizumab MOA review)
Expected immunological effect of cytokine modulation; extramedullary hematopoiesis may contribute.
